title: The Pursuit of Sustainable Development
text: Sustainable development has emerged as one of the most critical issues of our time. It refers to meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. This concept encompasses three fundamental pillars: economic growth, social inclusion and environmental protection. In recent years, many countries have made significant commitments to reducing carbon emissions and transitioning to renewable energy sources. However, the implementation of these policies often faces obstacles. Economic interests sometimes conflict with environmental objectives, creating a complex dilemma for policymakers. For instance, developing nations argue that they should not be held to the same environmental standards as wealthy countries that have already benefited from industrialisation. Despite these challenges, there is a growing consensus that sustainable practices are not merely ethical choices but also economic necessities. Companies that ignore sustainability risk losing consumer trust and market share. Moreover, technological innovations have made green alternatives more accessible and affordable than ever before. As citizens of a shared planet, we all have a role to play in fostering a sustainable future. Individual actions, when multiplied across millions of people, can drive substantial change.

questions.question: What does sustainable development refer to?
options: Meeting present needs without harming future generations

questions.question: What are the three pillars of sustainable development?
options: Economic growth, social inclusion and environmental protection

questions.question: What argument do developing nations make regarding environmental standards?
options: They should not be held to the same standards as wealthy countries
They don't believe in climate change
They have already met all standards

questions.question: What risk do companies face if they ignore sustainability?
options: Losing consumer trust and market share

questions.question: What does the writer say about individual actions?
options: They can drive substantial change when multiplied

questions.question: What has made green alternatives more accessible according to the passage?

title: The Role of Literature in Understanding Humanity
text: Literature has always served as a mirror to society, reflecting our collective hopes, fears and aspirations. From ancient epics to contemporary novels, stories have the unique ability to transcend time and space, allowing readers to experience lives dramatically different from their own. This capacity for empathy cultivation is perhaps literature's most valuable contribution to human development. When we read a novel, we temporarily inhabit the consciousness of another person. We feel their joy, sorrow, frustration and triumph. This process nurtures our capacity for emotional intelligence and deepens our understanding of human nature. Moreover, literature challenges our assumptions and broadens our perspectives. A well-crafted narrative can expose the flaws in our thinking and encourage us to reconsider deeply held beliefs. In an era dominated by quick information and superficial engagement, literature demands patience and reflection. It rewards those who invest time with profound insights that cannot be obtained through any other medium. For students of English, reading literary works also provides exposure to sophisticated vocabulary and complex sentence structures, enhancing both linguistic competence and cultural awareness.

questions.question: What does the writer say literature reflects?
What is literature's most valuable contribution according to the passage?
What happens when we read a novel according to the passage?
What does literature demand in an era of quick information?
What benefit does reading literature provide for English students?
What does literature challenge according to the passage?
